name: Chicken-Corn Casserole (Usda)
recipeIngredient: 48 ounces whole kernel corn, drained (3 cans)
6 ounces processed cheddar cheese, shredded (1 1/2 cups)
recipeInstructions:
1. Line four 8x8x2-inch baking pans with heat-resistant freezer wrap, leaving extra to fold over the top. Use one pan for each six servings or one-fourth of the recipe. Do not line pans if food will be served without freezing.
2. Melt fat. Stir in flour, salt, pepper, and onion. Add broth slowly, cooking until thickened, stirring constantly.
3. Mix hot mixture with chicken, corn, cheese, and pimentos. Pour one-fourth of the mixture into each baking pan, spreading well to avoid air pockets.
4. To serve without freezing: Preheat oven to 350°F (moderate). For each pan to be baked right away, mix 1/4 cup fine dry bread crumbs with 1 tablespoon melted butter or margarine. Sprinkle over chicken mixture in the baking pan. Bake for 45 minutes or until crumbs are lightly browned.
5. To freeze: Cool for 30 minutes at room temperature. Complete wrapping by pulling paper up over top of food. Fold edges together several times so paper lies directly on top of food. Fold ends of freezer wrap over the top and seal with freezer tape. Label with name of food, date of freezing, and last date for best eating quality (about 6 months). Freeze immediately for 10 to 12 hours before removing packages from pans.
6. To heat frozen food: Preheat oven to 350°F (moderate). Remove freezer wrap. Place food in baking pan. For each pan to be baked, mix 1/4 cup fine dry bread crumbs with 1 tablespoon melted butter or margarine. Sprinkle over chicken mixture in the baking pan. Bake for 1 1/4 hours or until crumbs are lightly browned and center is hot.
